statement.markdown:
Implement a `insert` method in your Trie class to handle string insertions efficiently.

When inserting a word, ensure that the word's path in the Trie is correctly constructed and updated.

Given a string word, insert it into your Trie.

Note: You may assume that the input string only contains lowercase English letters.
statement.inputFormat: A string input representing the word to be inserted.
statement.outputFormat: The inserted word itself to confirm it was correctly inserted.
statement.notes: You are expected to handle the edge case where the input word is an empty string.

examples.explanation: Inserts the word into the Trie.
Inserts the word into the Trie.
Inserts an empty string into the Trie.

hints.text: First hint text: Verify the implementation of the Trie's `insert` method for correct logic.

testCases.input: First line count, second line elements (stdin match)
